officialSources.title: GitHub Copilot individual plans: Introducing flex allotments
Models and pricing for GitHub Copilot
GitHub Copilot is moving to usage-based billing
Updates to GitHub Copilot billing and plans

github/gpt-5.5.description: Powerful model available in Copilot. Input: $5.00/MTok (≤272K), $10.00/MTok (Long context). Output: $30.00/MTok (Default), $45.00/MTok (Long context).
github/gpt-5.5.notes: Long context tier (>272K tokens) available at higher rates. Cached input: $0.50/MTok.

github/gpt-5.4.description: Versatile model. Input: $2.50/MTok (≤272K), $5.00/MTok (Long context). Output: $15.00/MTok (Default), $22.50/MTok (Long context).
github/gpt-5.4.notes: Long context tier (>272K tokens) available at higher rates. Cached input: $0.25/MTok.

copilot.description: AI-powered coding assistant with code completion, chat, and agentic capabilities.

code-completion.description: Inline code suggestions. Unlimited on paid plans. Does not consume AI Credits.[reference:0][reference:1]

next-edit-suggestions.description: Predictive next-edit suggestions. Unlimited on paid plans. Does not consume AI Credits.[reference:2][reference:3]

copilot-chat.description: Chat interface in IDE, GitHub.com, and CLI. Consumes AI Credits based on token usage.

cloud-agent.description: Autonomous cloud-based coding agent capable of running long, multi-step coding sessions.[reference:4][reference:5]

agent-mode.description: Agentic mode for autonomous task execution. Available on paid plans.[reference:6]

code-review.description: AI-powered code review. Consumes GitHub AI Credits and GitHub Actions minutes.[reference:7][reference:8]

copilot-cli.description: Copilot access from the command line.

copilot-mobile.description: Copilot access from GitHub Mobile app.

third-party-agents.description: Access to third-party agent integrations (public preview).[reference:9]

model-selection.description: Choice of AI models including GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, GPT-5.3-Codex, and more.

organization-management.description: Centralized management and policy controls for organization members.[reference:10]

policy-management.description: Copilot policy controls at the organization level.

audit-logs.description: Enterprise-grade audit logging for Copilot usage.

knowledge-base.description: Enterprise knowledge indexing for custom model context.[reference:11]

custom-models.description: Ability to deploy and use custom models.

ai-credits.description: Usage-based billing credits for Copilot. 1 AI credit = $0.01 USD.[reference:12]

user-budgets.description: Granular spend controls for organizations and enterprises. GA as of June 1, 2026.[reference:13]

plans.notes: 2,000 code completions/month.[reference:14] 50 chat/agent messages/month.[reference:15][reference:16] Limited access to select models.[reference:17]

$10/month. Includes $10 base credits + $5 flex allotment = $15 total included usage.[reference:18][reference:19] Code completions and next-edit suggestions unlimited and do not consume credits.[reference:20]
$39/month. Includes $39 base credits + $31 flex allotment = $70 total included usage.[reference:21][reference:22] Access to premium models.[reference:23]

$100/month. Includes $100 base credits + $100 flex allotment = $200 total included usage.[reference:24][reference:25] Designed for sustained, high-volume Copilot work.[reference:26][reference:27] Available for upgrade to existing Copilot subscribers as of June 1, 2026.[reference:28]
$19/user/month.[reference:29][reference:30] Per-user AI Credits pooled at the billing entity level.[reference:31] Includes centralized management and policy controls.[reference:32]

$39/user/month.[reference:33][reference:34] Priority access to new models and features.[reference:35] Larger monthly pool of AI credits.[reference:36] Enterprise-grade capabilities including knowledge indexing and custom models.[reference:37]

aiCredits.description: Usage is calculated based on token consumption (input, output, and cached tokens) using the listed per-model rates.[reference:44][reference:45] Code completions and next-edit suggestions do not consume credits.[reference:46]

creditAllotments.description: Pro: $15 total ($10 base + $5 flex). Pro+: $70 total ($39 base + $31 flex). Max: $200 total ($100 base + $100 flex).[reference:47] Business and Enterprise: per-user pooled credits.[reference:48]

additionalUsage.description: When included usage is exceeded, additional usage is billed in AI Credits at per-token rates.[reference:49] Individual plans may have additional usage limits.[reference:50]

codeReview.description: Consumes GitHub AI Credits and GitHub Actions minutes.[reference:51][reference:52]

userBudgets.description: Granular spend controls for organizations and enterprises. GA as of June 1, 2026.[reference:53]

apiPricing.notes: Base plan pricing unchanged as of June 1, 2026.[reference:54] Flex allotments are variable and may change over time.[reference:55] New sign-ups for Pro, Pro+, Max, and Student are temporarily paused.[reference:56]

notes: GitHub Copilot transitioned to usage-based billing on June 1, 2026.[reference:57] Premium request units (PRUs) replaced by GitHub AI Credits.[reference:58] Code completions and next-edit suggestions remain unlimited on paid plans and do not consume credits.[reference:59] 1 AI credit = $0.01 USD.[reference:60] Flex allotments are variable and designed to adapt as the economics of AI evolve.[reference:61]
